whatWasImplemented: supabase/migrations/20260419070000_escalation_rules.sql — escalation_rules table with FK cascade from sites, rule_type CHECK ('turn_count'|'keyword'|'intent'), config jsonb with jsonb_typeof CHECK object-only, action CHECK ('ask_email'|'ask_phone'|'show_form'|'calendly_link'|'handoff'), action_config jsonb object-only, priority int default 0, is_active boolean default true.
Compound index (site_id, is_active, priority DESC, created_at ASC) mirrors M6F1's custom_responses hot-path index — same runtime ordering.
Four owner-scoped RLS policies (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) resolve through sites.user_id = auth.uid(). Widget never reads directly; runtime evaluator uses service-role client.

conversations.needs_human boolean column added with default false. Partial index conversations_needs_human_idx on (site_id) WHERE needs_human IS TRUE keeps the flag scan fast for the dashboard conversation list without inflating the full table index.

src/__tests__/escalation-schema.test.ts — 14 integration tests: defaults, rule_type / action CHECK violations, all 5 actions accepted, keyword + intent rule types, owner SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE, peer isolation (A can't pwn B's priority), anon reads zero, ON DELETE CASCADE from sites, needs_human default + UPDATE persists.
whatWasLeftUndone: No assertions fulfilled directly — foundation for M7F2/F3/F4.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 506 passed / 1 skipped / 63 files (+14 new).

manualChecks.action: User A UPDATE of user B's rule priority
